questions.question: Define wetlands and list four major types.
keyPoints: Wetlands are transitional zones between terrestrial and aquatic environments with standing water or saturated soils
Four types: marshes, swamps, bogs, fens

questions.question: Explain why wetlands are called 'kidneys of the landscape'.
keyPoints: Similar function to kidneys filtering blood

questions.question: Differentiate between bogs and fens based on water source and acidity.
keyPoints: Bogs: fed by precipitation (ombrotrophic), more acidic (pH 3-5)
Fens: fed by groundwater (minerotrophic), less acidic
Bogs dominated by sphagnum moss; fens by grasses and sedges

questions.question: List three ecological functions of wetlands.
What is the Ramsar Convention? When and where was it signed?
keyPoints: Aims at conservation and wise use of wetlands

questions.question: Describe three major threats to wetlands.
keyPoints: Pollution from industrial and agricultural sources

questions.question: Explain the role of wetlands in climate change mitigation.
keyPoints: Store large amounts of carbon in soils
Help regulate local and global climate
